mission.title: valkey: Support smooth swithover in standalone mode by using REDIRECT
mission.description: Support smooth swithover in standalone mode by using REDIRECT. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current valkey setup
steps.description:
Verify your valkey version and configuration:
```bash
valkey-server --version
```
This feature requires a working valkey installation.

steps.title: Review valkey configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant valkey configuration:
Issue: https://github.com/redis/redis/issues/12097
PR: https://github.com/redis/redis/pull/12192

In many scenarios, we need to perform the action of switching.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support smooth swithover in standalone mode by using…
steps.description:
To implement #319 

1. replica is able to redirect read and write commands to it's primary in standalone mode
    * reply with "-REDIRECT primary-ip:port"
2. add a subcommand `CLIENT CAPA redirect`, a client can announce the capability to handle redirection
    * if a client can handle redirection, the data access commands (read and write) will be redirected
3. allow `readonly` and `readwrite` command in standalone mode, may be a breaking change
    * a client with redirect capability cannot

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Support smooth swithover in standalone mode by using…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
To implement #319 

1. replica is able to redirect read and write commands to it's primary in standalone mode
    * reply with "-REDIRECT primary-ip:port"
2. add a subcommand `CLIENT CAPA redirect`, a client can announce the capability to handle redirection
    * if a client can handle redirection, the data access commands (read and write) will be redirected
3.

prerequisites.description: A working valkey installation or development environment.
